requirements.title: Completion and Continuation Routes
requirements.description: The map must represent stopping points plus optional continuation routes such as quality and knowledge capture.

requirements.title: Scenario Model
requirements.description: The app must use a scenario-oriented model with steps, choices, active state, terminal options, and transition metadata.

requirements.title: Scenario Slicing
requirements.description: The map should render one active scenario slice at a time rather than one global graph.

constraints.constraint: The MVP must show closing/continuation choices but should not expand downstream quality or knowledge pipeline internals.
constraints.rationale: This preserves optional routes without overloading the first map.

constraints.constraint: The MVP must introduce scenario-oriented data instead of relying only on selected node state.
constraints.rationale: Stepwise command choice requires active step, choices, and terminal options.

constraints.constraint: Scenario command facts must align with the `maestro-flow` submodule.
constraints.rationale: The submodule is the source of truth for command-chain semantics.

constraints.constraint: The MVP should explain output conditions but must not require real `status.json` parsing.
constraints.rationale: The first product is an educational wiki, not an execution-state assistant.

domain.problem_statement: The requirements need to avoid treating command choice as a single recommendation; users need to understand command sequences, optional exits, and continuation routes.
domain.audience: Internal validators and future users learning Maestro command selection.

terminology.definition: Data model that groups workflow nodes, edges, steps, current step, available choices, and terminal options for one user scenario.
Optional route from a closing node into another workflow path such as quality checks or knowledge capture.
A focused subset of the workflow map for one user scenario, avoiding an unreadable global graph.

non_goals.title: Deep downstream pipeline expansion in MVP
non_goals.rationale: The first version only shows continuation branch choices.

non_goals.title: Real execution-state parsing in MVP
non_goals.rationale: The first version explains output conditions without parsing `status.json`.

insights.summary: Current UI supports node selection but lacks scenario selection, path highlighting, stepwise choices, terminal options, and cross-scenario transitions.

open_questions.question: Which multi-role set should elaborate the next brainstorm?
Which exact upstream `maestro-flow` files should be cited for each command path?
